# DomyDomains - Domain Search Platform
> The fastest domain name search tool on the internet. Search 400+ domain extensions instantly at https://domydomains.com

## What is DomyDomains?
DomyDomains is a free domain name search and discovery platform that lets users search over 400 domain extensions (TLDs) instantly. It includes tools for domain generation, premium domain browsing, domain pricing comparison, WHOIS lookup guidance, and domain value estimation. The platform aggregates pricing from multiple registrars so users can find and register domains at the best price. DomyDomains is designed for entrepreneurs, developers, startups, and businesses looking for the perfect domain name.

## Core Tools
- **Domain Search** (https://domydomains.com) — Search 400+ TLDs instantly with real-time availability and pricing
- **Domain Extensions Guide** (https://domydomains.com/domain-extensions) — Complete guide to all TLDs including .com, .ai, .io, .dev
- **Domain Name Generator** (https://domydomains.com/domain-generator) — Generate creative, brandable domain name ideas from keywords
- **Premium Domains** (https://domydomains.com/premium-domains) — Browse aftermarket domains with traffic, backlinks, and authority
- **Domain Pricing Guide** (https://domydomains.com/domain-pricing) — Compare registration and renewal costs across registrars
- **WHOIS Lookup Guide** (https://domydomains.com/whois-lookup) — Check domain ownership, registration dates, and expiration
- **Domain Value Guide** (https://domydomains.com/domain-value) — Estimate domain worth based on length, keywords, TLD, and SEO metrics

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much does a domain name cost?
Domain names typically cost $8-15/year for standard extensions like .com, .net, and .org. Premium TLDs like .ai cost $50-80/year, while .io runs $30-50/year. Budget extensions like .xyz start at $1-2/year for the first year. Premium aftermarket domains can range from $100 to millions depending on length, keywords, and brandability.

### What is the best domain extension for a startup?
For most startups, .com remains the gold standard due to universal trust and recognition. If your .com is taken, .io is popular with tech startups, .ai is ideal for AI/ML companies, .co is a professional short alternative, and .dev works well for developer tools. The best choice depends on your industry and target audience.

### How do I check if a domain name is available?
Use DomyDomains at https://domydomains.com to search any domain name across 400+ extensions instantly. The tool shows real-time availability, pricing from multiple registrars, and suggests alternatives if your first choice is taken.
